trust_report.doctrine: null-over-guess; deterministic fields anchored to CDS/Scorecard with provenance; high-stakes fields blind-verified; arithmetic-gated; every source consulted is logged

overall_acceptance_rate.quote: C1: 'Total first-time, first-year who applied 54008' / 'Total first-time, first-year who were admitted 1970' => 1970/54008 = 3.6% (corroborated by College Scorecard ADM_RATE=0.0365 = 3.65%).
overall_acceptance_rate.as_of_cycle: 2024-2025 (Fall 2024 entering class); target 2027-2028 CDS not yet published

in_state_acceptance_rate.quote: C1 residency breakdown (Fall 2024): applied In-State 3032, admitted In-State 265 => 265/3032 = 8.7%.

out_of_state_acceptance_rate.quote: C1 residency breakdown (Fall 2024): applied Out-of-State 34216, admitted Out-of-State 1380 => 1380/34216 = 4.0%.

transfer_acceptance_rate.quote: D2 Transfer Admission (Fall 2024): Total Applicants 2,256, Admitted 16, Enrolled 13 => 16/2,256 = 0.7%.

international_acceptance_rate.quote: C1 residency breakdown (Fall 2024): applied International 16760, admitted International 325 => 325/16760 = 1.9%.

applications_total.quote: C1: 'Total first-time, first-year who applied 54008' (men 25613 + women 28372 + another gender 23).

admits_total.quote: C1: 'Total first-time, first-year who were admitted 1970' (men 920 + women 1050 + another gender 0).

enrolled_total.quote: C1: 'Total first-time, first-year who enrolled 1647' (full-time men 767 + women 880).

yield_rate.quote: C1: enrolled 1647 / admitted 1970 = 83.6% (yield; not printed in CDS, derived from the two C1 cells).

gpa_weighted_avg.quote: C12: average high school GPA = '4.21' (99.21% of enrolled first-year students submitted GPA). A 4.21 average exceeds 4.0, i.e. a weighted scale.

race_white.quote: B2 (Degree-Seeking First-Time First-Year): 'White, non-Hispanic 457'; total first-year 1,641 => 457/1641 = 27.8%.

race_black.quote: B2 (First-Time First-Year): 'Black or African American, non-Hispanic 112'; total 1,641 => 112/1641 = 6.8%.

race_native_american.quote: B2 (First-Time First-Year): 'American Indian or Alaska Native, non-Hispanic 3'; total 1,641 => 3/1641 = 0.2%.

race_pacific_islander.quote: B2 (First-Time First-Year): 'Native Hawaiian or other Pacific Islander, non-Hispanic 2'; total 1,641 => 2/1641 = 0.1%.

race_two_or_more.quote: B2 (First-Time First-Year): 'Two or more races, non-Hispanic 127'; total 1,641 => 127/1641 = 7.7%.

race_unknown.quote: B2 (First-Time First-Year): 'Race and/or ethnicity unknown 117'; total 1,641 => 117/1641 = 7.1%.

international_percentage.quote: B2 Nonresidents: first-year 265 of 1,641 = 16.1%. (Alt. degree-seeking undergrad: 'Nonresidents 1,048' of 6,979 = 15.0%.)

freshman_retention_rate.quote: B22: full-time first-year cohort entering Fall 2023, percentage enrolled as of official Fall 2024 enrollment = '98%'.

graduation_rate_4_year.quote: Section B grad-rate grid, Fall 2018 cohort: D 'completed in four years or less (by Aug. 31, 2022)' Total = 920; C 'final cohort after adjusting for allowable exclusions' Total = 1653 => 920/1653 = 55.7%. Independently confirmed by College Scorecard C100_4 = 0.5566.

graduation_rate_6_year.quote: Section B: 'Six-year graduation rate for 2018 cohort (G divided by C)' Total = '98%'. Confirmed by College Scorecard C150_4 = 0.9758.

in_state_tuition.quote: G1 PRIVATE INSTITUTIONS 'Tuition: $59,320' (First-Year and Undergraduates). Private institution: single tuition, no in-state/out-of-state distinction.

out_of_state_tuition.quote: G1 PRIVATE INSTITUTIONS 'Tuition: $59,320'. Private institution charges the same tuition regardless of residency.

total_coa_in_state.quote: Sum of stated G components (2025-2026): Tuition $59,320 + Required Fees $5,476 + Food and housing $22,130 + Books and supplies $1,000 + Other expenses $2,500 = $90,426 (Transportation listed as 'varies', excluded). CDS prints no single COA total.

total_coa_out_of_state.quote: Private institution: same as in-state. Sum of G components (2025-2026): $59,320 + $5,476 + $22,130 + $1,000 + $2,500 = $90,426 (Transportation 'varies', excluded).

median_earnings_10yr.source: U.S. Dept of Education College Scorecard (Most-Recent-Cohorts-Institution, 2026-06-10 release)
median_earnings_10yr.quote: College Scorecard institution data file (UNITID 166027, Harvard University): md_earn_wne_p10 = 101817 (median earnings of students working and not enrolled, 10 years after entry). Retrieved from ed-public-download.scorecard.network/downloads/Most-Recent-Cohorts-Institution_06102026.zip.

is_test_optional.quote: C8A: for 'SAT or ACT' the checkbox is under column 1 = 'Required to be considered for admission'. C8: 'Does your institution make use of SAT or ACT scores in admission decisions...?' = Yes. (Note: the enrolled Fall 2024 class was admitted under a test-optional policy - only 54% submitted SAT / 19% ACT; the required-testing policy applies to subsequent applicants.)
is_test_optional.as_of_cycle: 2024-2025 CDS (policy for applicants applying for Fall 2026)

test_policy_details.value: SAT or ACT 'Required to be considered for admission' (C8A); institution uses SAT/ACT in admission decisions (C8=Yes); scores due 3/1/25 (C8E). Enrolled Fall 2024 class was admitted test-optional (54% submitted SAT, 19% ACT).
test_policy_details.quote: C8 'make use of SAT or ACT scores in admission decisions' = Yes; C8A 'SAT or ACT' = 'Required to be considered for admission'; C8E 'Latest date by which SAT or ACT scores must be received for fall-term' = 3/1/25.

Surfaced in search — older 2023-2024 CDS, superseded; not the target cycle.
Actual PDF bytes (redirect target) downloaded and text-extracted with pypdf (layout mode). Source of C1/C2/C8/C9/C12/B2/B22/B-grad/D2/G/C21-C22 cell values.
The actual canonical CDS 2024-2025 PDF (Harvard-hosted CDN). Extracted Section C1 verbatim: Fall 2024, 54,008 applied / 1,970 admitted. Source of the reported value.

"Common Data Set 2024-2025 ... C1 First-time, first-year students: Provide the number of degree-seeking, first-time, first-year students who applied, were admitted, and enrolled (full- or part-time) in Fall 2024. ... Total first-time, first-year who applied 54008 ... Total first-time, first-year who were admitted 1970". (The CDS states raw counts only; it does not print an admit-rate percentage. 1,970 / 54,008 = 3.65%.)

Actual 2024-2025 CDS PDF (also at oira.harvard.edu/files/2025/06/HarvardUniversity_CDS_2024-2025.pdf). Extracted C9: SAT Composite row BLANK; only SAT EBRW (740/760/780) and SAT Math (770/790/800) reported. Cohort = enrolled Fall 2024. No SAT total middle-50%.
Actual file served after redirect; extracted text locally with pypdf. Header confirms 'Common Data Set 2024-2025'; B22 Retention Rates = 98% for Fall 2023 cohort.
PRIMARY SOURCE. Fetched and text-extracted (pypdf). Section B contains the verbatim six-year graduation rate line for the 2018 cohort: Total = 98% (1613/1653).
Fetched. Only a generic example query (Marketing Manager roles in Washington DC) with an example employer list unrelated to Harvard graduate placements; not usable as Harvard top employers.
Harvard FAS Mignone Center official archive, but only covers Class of 2011-2019 (behind a PDF). Too stale for the target cycle; no employer list on the page.

Alternate fields-of-study page. Only restated the '50 undergraduate fields' and '50 secondary fields' blurb; did NOT enumerate concentration names or degree types. Used only to corroborate the '50 secondary fields' count claim; not a source for the list itself.
majors/catalog page
Official Harvard College concentrations (majors) listing page. Reported as majors_catalog_url.

PRIMARY catalog page named in the task. Quoted the intro ('more than 3,700 courses in 50 undergraduate fields of study, which we call concentrations') and the verbatim concentration list with degree types for A-through-Philosophy (e.g., 'Mechanical Engineering (B.S.)', 'Electrical Engineering (B.A., B.S.)'). Page content truncated after Philosophy on both fetches, so P-Z degree types were not confirmed here.
official admissions page
Official Harvard College undergraduate admissions page (returned as top official result). Reported as official_admissions_url.

Official. Quoted deadlines 'November 1 - Restrictive Early Action' (notified mid-December) and 'January 1 - Regular Decision' (notified end of March); Common Application; SAT/ACT required; five 150-word short-answer essays.

Harvard College admissions-statistics page. Reports Class of 2029 (Fall 2025): 47,893 applicants, 2,003 admitted (~4.2%), no stated percentage. Rejected: not a CDS, does not state a rate, and still not the Fall 2027 target — a more recent but non-canonical, non-target cycle.
Harvard College admissions stats page (search result); not fetched; not the canonical CDS and no Fall 2027 data.
Harvard admissions-statistics page appeared in results; not fetched/quoted, so not used (Crimson figures used instead).
Search result only — general admissions stats; not relevant to major/house-ranking tactics.
Official. Common Application; '$90 fee (or request a fee waiver)'; REA Nov 1, RD Jan 1, Transfer Mar 1; no cycle year printed.

Official. 'We accept the Common Application' + 'We are also a QuestBridge partner institution'; SAT/ACT requirement + self-report; two teacher recs; secondary school report; midyear report required; five short-answers 150-word limit; fee waiver language.

Checked to find an explicit 'admission is to the College, not a major' statement; the fetched content did not contain such a sentence, so I relied on the third-term-declaration quote instead. Rejected as a direct source.

Official. REA is 'non-binding'; restriction language (may not apply to other private ED/EA/REA; may apply early to public/service-academy/non-US non-binding, and RD/ED II elsewhere); Nov 1 / Jan 1 deadlines and notification timing.

Official Harvard College page. Source of transfer restrictions: max 16 semester-long courses, accredited full-time daytime program, grade C or better, and the full ineligible-course list (including AP/IB/CLEP not accepted). Verbatim quotes captured.
Official list of activities available at Harvard (not admit-profile opinion); used as background for common_activities themes, not as a community source.
Attempted for a housing quote; returned HTTP 404. Not usable.
financial-aid page
Official Harvard College financial aid landing page. Reported as financial_aid_url.

Anchor page. Philosophy ('your financial circumstances will not keep you from Harvard'), need-based only, international students same basis, 25% pay nothing / 55% receive Harvard scholarship, ~$13k avg parent contribution, $100k/$200k income tiers.
Harvard College's own cost page; pinned $59,320 to AY 2025-2026 and gave latest-published AY 2026-2027 tuition = $62,226. No 2027-2028 figure.
Need-blind + 100% of need; 'There are no merit-based awards'; income thresholds; home equity/retirement excluded; summer work expectation eliminated 2020-21; $13k avg parent contribution.
HTTP 404 — page not found. Data recovered from other pages instead.
HTTP 404 — page not found.
Named grants: Harvard Scholarship (grant, never repaid), Federal Pell, SEOG, Massachusetts Gilbert Grant; plus work-study and loan details. Source for the scholarships array quotes.
Official Harvard tips surfaced via search; not community opinion, so used only as light corroboration, not cited as a crowdsourced source.
'55% of our undergraduates receive need-based Harvard scholarships'; typical 2023-24 package ($68,700 scholarship, $12,800 parent contribution); ~20% Pell of ~6,700 undergrads.
Official Harvard College FAQ. Source of the philosophy, AP general_rule, IB general_rule, diploma_bonus=false, and the language-requirement exception. Verbatim quote captured.

Official Harvard College FAQ. Source for transfer_articulation.tools=[]: no transferable-course list is kept; credit evaluated individually by the Registrar's Office after admission. Verbatim quote captured.
House-system small-college-within-a-research-university framing; used in social_environment.
Harvard College page; source for MBTA subway at Harvard Square, ~15-min ride to downtown Boston, Bluebikes 400+ stations + discount; used in transportation_impact.

Basis for housing_profile randomization detail.

Fetched (official Harvard). Confirmed Houses are randomly sorted via blocking groups in February of sophomore year, 'luck of the draw', no application-time ranking — basis for empty college_ranking_tactics.

source_ledger.url: https://www.google.com/search (WebSearch: 'Harvard University College Scorecard median earnings 10 years after entry 166027')
notes: Surfaced aggregators (collegesimply, decidemycampus, cnbc, ivycoach) citing ~$101,817 / older $95,114; used only to sanity-check the ballpark, not as the reported value.
WebSearch. Corroborated Nov 1 REA / Jan 1 RD and surfaced the official Harvard apply/requirements URLs; no numeric values taken from third-party summaries.
WebSearch. Surfaced the official CDS 2024-2025 PDF URL and the OIRA publication post.
WebSearch. Confirmed no 2025-2026/2027-2028 CDS for Harvard; third-party C7 summaries (gradgpt, admitreport, accoladetutor, etc.) not used — relied on the official PDF instead.

WebSearch for the 2027-2028 CDS; confirmed no 2027-2028 edition exists; returned only secondary blog aggregators (legacyonlineschool, accoladetutor, collegefactual, research.com) which were not used as sources.
WebSearch surfaced official OIR/OIRA CDS pages plus secondary aggregators (gradgpt, ameriinsight, scribd); used only to locate the official OIRA index, not for the value itself.
Third-party CDS visualizer surfaced in search results; not fetched — secondary aggregator, non-canonical.
Third-party CDS visualizer titled '2026 Common Data Set'; not a primary source; rejected.
Third-party CDS aggregator; not canonical, not fetched for the figure.
Third-party CDS aggregator, non-canonical; not used.
Third-party CDS visualization; not used — official Harvard PDF used instead.
Harvard Graduate School of Education outcomes PDF; graduate school, out of scope for the College.
Harvard Magazine 2018 news article on the reduction of Advanced Standing. Contextual/historical, not a policy page; not used for values.
Reported Princeton/MIT/Harvard as 1-2-3 in U.S. News; NOT used because rank must come from a fetched U.S. News page, which failed to load.
Harvard Kennedy School (graduate school) employment snapshot; out of scope for undergraduate Harvard College outcomes.
Harvard Management Company; context on endowment management, not a specific figure used.
Search result only, not fetched — Quad-assignment analysis; enrolled-student housing detail, not applicant tactic.
Harvard Chan School of Public Health (graduate). Out of scope; not used.
Explainer on what Harvard looks for (academics + character/leadership/essays); used in admissions_philosophy.
Admissions-consultant blog; not crowdsourced; rejected.
Search result only, not fetched — about popularity of majors, not admissions-by-major mechanics; not needed.
Candidate source for top employers ('where they work' alumni aggregation), but LinkedIn alumni data is behind a login wall and not fetchable via WebFetch; could not verify any named employers.
Third-party SAT-data aggregator; not canonical; rejected.
General campus/student-life context; supported social_environment.
Same — surfaced via search summary only; used for culture theme, page not directly fetchable (403).
Student reviews describing an exclusive/competitive social scene and final clubs; used in campus_dynamics.social_environment.
Surfaced in search; not directly fetchable (Niche blocks crawler). Not independently used.
Page fetch FAILED (HTTP 403). Content used only via WebSearch summary (final clubs prominence, inclusion concerns) and clearly flagged as non-verbatim paraphrase.
Surfaced in search; Niche blocks crawler; not fetched.
Search result only, not fetched — general how-to-get-in overview; no specific concentration-tactic content needed.
Third-party (PrepScholar) ACT summary surfaced when checking College Scorecard as an alternate; not canonical, not used.
Fetched verbatim. Student-survey quotes: 'works really hard, doesn't sleep, involved in a million extracurriculars'; partying not prevalent.
U.S. News rankings press release; not a U.S. News ranking page fetch, so not used for rank.
Search result only, not fetched — Quora, lower reliability; the point (no major required) is already covered by the official Harvard and Admissionado sources.
Search result only, not fetched — Quora, lower reliability; not needed.
Fetch FAILED — 'Claude Code is unable to fetch from www.reddit.com'. No Reddit content obtainable.
Secondary blog surfaced in search; not fetched — non-canonical.
Third-party. Non-official; not used.
Scribd copy of the 2024-2025 CDS; used the official Harvard PDF instead.
Scribd re-host of the 2024-2025 CDS; used the official Harvard-hosted PDF instead.

Re-host of Harvard CDS on Scribd; not used — original Harvard PDF used.
Third-party; not used.

Crimson 2025 senior survey article in search results; not fetched separately (the features.thecrimson.com/2025 after-harvard page already provided the quoted figures).
Harvard Crimson journalism, not crowdsourced student opinion; not used for this section.

Search result only, not fetched — change to blocking/linking rules; enrolled-student housing mechanics, not applicant tactics.
Search result only — Class of 2027 admit-rate news; not about major-selection or house-ranking tactics.
The Harvard Crimson; source for Class of 2028 admit rate 3.6% (1,937 of 54,008) and 83.6% yield used in market_position and selectivity takeaway.
Admissions-explainer summarizing Harvard's holistic/whole-person criteria; used in admissions_philosophy.
Secondary U.S. News rank coverage; not used for the same reason (rank left null).
Fetch FAILED (HTTP 403). Crowdsourced student stereotypes not retrievable.
Attempted WebFetch for the National Universities rank; timed out (60s) on multiple tries. U.S. News blocks automated fetch, so rank could not be quoted -> us_news_rank left null.
Attempted WebFetch for the rank; timed out. Not usable.
